epigraph.text: By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.
epigraph.quote: Proportion tests arise naturally in polling, quality control, and medicine.

content.text: For categorical data, we test claims about population proportions.

content.title: $z$-Test for Proportion
content.content:
To test $H_0: p = p_0$ vs.\ $H_a$:
\[
 z = \frac{\phat - p_0}{\sqrt{p_0(1-p_0)/n}}
\]
Under $H_0$, $z \approx \Normal(0, 1)$ for large $n$.
content.label: Theorem

content.text:
Note: We use $p_0$ (not $\phat$) in the standard error because 
we're computing probability under $H_0$.

content.title: Test for Proportion
content.problem:
A coin is flipped 100 times, giving 60 heads. Test if it's fair 
($p = 0.5$) at $\alpha = 0.05$.
